title: ceratocephalus javanicus
intro: Ceratocephalus javanicus, also known as Java toothwort, is a plant species belonging to the Berberidaceae family, characterized by its unique, tooth-like leaf margins. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiviral effects. The health benefits of Ceratocephalus javanicus include its potential to treat fever, cough, and respiratory issues, as well as its use as an anti-diarrheal agent. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used to make teas, infusions, and decoctions, which can be consumed to harness its medicinal properties.
